summary:
Teeth begin their eruption process at or around Nolla’s Stage 6 when they show initial signs of moving toward the mouth but are not fully visible. Eruption continues through subsequent stages until completion.
 Your answer will be simplified and concise while retaining all essential medical facts for clarity and precision as per your requirements:

Teeth initiate their eruption starting from Nolla’s Stage 6, showing early movements into the oral cavity; full emergence occurs progressively thereafter. To summarize this clearly within two sentences focused only on critical aspects related directly to answering "at which stage does a tooth start erupting?":

**Tooth eruption begins during Nolla’s Stage 6, characterized by initiation of movement towards the oral cavity before becoming visibly erupted.**
This summary captures both the timing and nature of the first sign indicating an upcoming eruption event based strictly upon provided context regarding dental development milestones outlined via Nollas' staging system.
